title: Visualizing the Diameter of a Set in a Metric Space
description: A diagram showing various sets with their diameters calculated based on the maximum distance between any two points within the set.
relationships: d(x,y) = diam(A) represents the diameter of bounded Set A
d(p,q) → ∞ shows that unbounded Set B has infinite diameter
The diameter is defined as sup{d(a,b) : a,b ∈ S} for any set S in the metric space
